policies.name: Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Schemes 2022 for Assistance to Industries (MSME / Large & Thrust Sector / Mega Industries)
policies.incentive_type: capital subsidy + interest subsidy + net SGST reimbursement + EPF reimbursement
policies.what_companies_get: Three GR-backed tracks verified on site: (1) 'Capital Subsidy to Large Industries and Thrust Sector' GR; (2) MSME assistance; (3) Mega Industries. From the official 'Draft Guidelines for Large and Thrust Sector' PDF: net SGST reimbursement with annual ceiling of 1.2% of eFCI (large) and 8% of eFCI (thrust/PEC-based track), interest subsidy claimable up to 9.5 years from DoCP, 90% of eligible net SGST disbursed at claim with balance later. Full rate matrix is in the GR PDFs (capital subsidy % of eFCI by taluka category I/II/III). Separate draft guideline PDFs exist for MSMEs, Large & Thrust, and Mega.
policies.status: operative (GRs of 2022 with 2023-2024 guideline updates and 2024 reconstitution of sanctioning authorities)
policies.application_status: live -- applications via ifp.gujarat.gov.in Common Application Form; portal has an 'Incentive to Industry Scheme' module, CAF Incentives Manual, user manuals, and a public 'Incentives Granted Dashboard'
policies.site_notes: Guidelines still labelled 'Draft' on the site; scheme GRs (e.g. GR No. MIS-102020-327024-I capital subsidy, taluka classification GR MIS-102020-347965-I) are downloadable.

policies.name: Maharashtra Industries, Investment and Services Policy 2025 (MIISP 2025) -- successor to Package Scheme of Incentives (PSI) 2019
policies.incentive_type: umbrella industrial incentive policy (industrial promotion subsidy, interest subsidy, capex support)
policies.what_companies_get: GR dated 31-12-2025 hosted on MAITRI. GR is in Marathi; fragments extracted: industrial-park support of 55% or Rs 250 crore (whichever lower), 50% capital subsidy for ETPs (cap ~Rs 10 crore), 20% of solar-project cost (cap ~Rs 4.8 crore), 3% subsidised-interest loans for entrepreneurs, targets of 27% GSVA industry share by 2036. Full incentive schedule requires reading the Marathi GR. PSI-2019 GR ('Maharashtra New Industrial Policy 2019' + IPS-GST modification GR) still hosted alongside.
policies.application_status: MAITRI single-window portal live with an online 'Incentive calculator' and PSI beneficiaries GIS map
policies.site_notes: This is the 'Industrial Policy 2025' the brief speculated about; official name confirmed.

policies.name: Rajasthan Investment Promotion Scheme 2024 (RIPS 2024), updated 16 May 2025
policies.incentive_type: asset creation incentive (choice of investment subsidy / capital subsidy / turnover-linked incentive) + top-up boosters + duty exemptions
policies.what_companies_get: Verified from the official state PDF: Asset Creation Incentive is a one-time, irreversible choice among (a) Investment Subsidy: reimbursement of 75% of State tax (SGST) due and deposited for 7 years; (b) Capital Subsidy: 13% to 28% of EFCI depending on project category and area category, disbursed in annual instalments over 10 years post commercial production; (c) Turnover Linked Incentive: 1.20% to 2.00% of Net Sales Turnover annually for 10 years, subject to annual ceilings. Top-ups: Employment Booster (e.g. lifts TLI 1.4%->1.54%), plus one of Thrust Booster / Anchor Booster / 5% Interest Subvention on term loans. Exemptions: 100% electricity duty for 7 years; 100% mandi/market fee reimbursement 7 years; 75% stamp duty exemption + 25% reimbursement; 75% land conversion charge exemption + 25% reimbursement; 5%-point VAT reimbursement on PNG for 7 years. Land: 25% upfront + balance 75% over 10 years at 8% interest. Sunrise sectors (sec 3.3) get an additional booster; separate manufacturing and services thrust-sector lists (secs 9.4/9.5).
policies.application_status: RajNivesh one-stop-shop portal is live (rajnivesh.rajasthan.gov.in responded, title 'Home - RAJNIVESH'); dedicated rips.rajasthan.gov.in REFUSED connections
policies.site_notes: Incentive numbers come from the official Government of Rajasthan PDF on finance.rajasthan.gov.in since the RIPS portal itself was down.

Caveat verified on the Interest Subsidy page: the INTEREST SUBSIDY SCHEME 2008 notification (No. 3/40/2003/IND) states validity only up to 31 March 2011 yet is still presented as a current service -- currency of several component schemes is doubtful.

policies.site_notes: Site is Drupal, valid content but expired/mismatched TLS certificate (fetch required -k).

GUJARAT: IP-2020 sub-schemes still operative with 2025-2026 guideline updates -- Integrated Logistics Facilities assistance (revised 2025), Environment Protection Measures Schemes 1-2 (2026 guidelines), R&D Assistance (2025 operational guidelines), Industrial Park / Logistic Park schemes, Industrial Infrastructure assistance
GUJARAT: Garment and Apparel Policy 2017 live registration module on IFP; public 'Incentives Granted Dashboard' on IFP
MAHARASHTRA: Maharashtra Industries, Investment and Services Policy 2025 (GR 31-12-2025) -- the actual PSI-2019 successor

MAHARASHTRA: Global Capability Centre (GCC) Policy 2025; AVGC-XR Policy 2025; MATRIX Policy 2025; Gems & Jewellery Policy 2025; Bamboo Industry Policy 2025; Maharashtra Logistics Policy 2024; State Export Promotion Policy 2023; Special Amnesty Scheme for closed units; Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar SC/ST special incentive package; online Incentive Calculator on MAITRI
MAHARASHTRA: IT/ITeS policy live version is 2024 (brief said 2023); EV policy live version is 2021 (brief said 2025)

RAJASTHAN: RIPS 2024 top-up architecture beyond brief -- Employment Booster, Thrust/Anchor Boosters, 5% Interest Subvention, PNG VAT reimbursement, mandi-fee reimbursement, 25%-upfront land payment model; RajNivesh portal as the live one-stop shop

GOA: Employment Subsidy Scheme for Cashew Industry; amended Umbrella Scheme; Target Schemes; Preferential Purchase Incentives for MSEs; State Export Strategy 2021; evidence that Interest Subsidy Scheme 2008 formally lapsed 31-03-2011 while still listed
